panels.title: CMS calls (24h)
panels.description: Every outbound HTTP request via api/http_cache.py. Excludes anthropic.messages.create.

panels.title: CMS failures (24h)
panels.description: outcome=failure on any non-anthropic api.call. Spike = a CMS is down.

panels.title: Cache hits (24h)
panels.description: requests_cache returned a cached response → no upstream call. Higher = cheaper, faster.

panels.title: Cache misses (24h)
panels.description: Real upstream requests. Sustained high count for a single host = cache TTL too short or misconfigured.

panels.title: Calls per hour, top targets
panels.description: topk(10) target paths over the period. Top entries should be the active CMSs (squadi, nbl1, geniussports, etc).

targets.expr: topk(10, sum by (target) (count_over_time({app="scout"} | json | event_type="api.call" | target!="anthropic.messages.create" [1h])))
topk(10, quantile_over_time(0.95, {app="scout"} | json | event_type="api.call" | target!="anthropic.messages.create" | unwrap duration_ms [10m]) by (target))

panels.title: Cache hit ratio by target (24h, top 20)
panels.description: Higher % = effective caching. 0% = TTL too short or always-uncached path. ~100% = TTL too long maybe.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (target, cache_hit) (count_over_time({app="scout"} | json | event_type="api.call" | target!="anthropic.messages.create" [24h])))

options.content:
**Not yet wired.** This panel needs a `scout.match.refreshed` cyclops event emitted from `api/BasketballGame.py:enrich_completed_game()` carrying `competition_id`, `fq_match_id`, `pbp_action_count`, and `data_source`. Once shipped, replace this text panel with:

```
sum by (competition_id) (
  count_over_time(
    {app="scout"} | json | event_type="scout.match.refreshed" [24h]
  )
)
```

as a stat-table, sorted descending. v1 of this dashboard uses the CMS-call panels above as a proxy — top targets reveal which CMSs are being hit, which is one layer removed from "games loaded".
